title: Key-Value Store (Redis Cloud)
description: Redis Cloud is a fully-managed Redis service with advanced data structures. Offers pub/sub, streams, sorted sets, and Redis modules (RedisJSON, RedisSearch). Best for: high-performance caching, real-time analytics, message queues, session stores. Provides managed infrastructure with automatic failover.

provides.reasoning: Redis Cloud is fundamentally a key-value store, providing fast in-memory data storage and retrieval as its core functionality.
Redis Cloud natively supports publish/subscribe messaging patterns, enabling real-time message distribution across multiple subscribers.
Redis Cloud excels as a high-performance caching layer due to its in-memory architecture, which is explicitly mentioned as a primary use case in the description.
The description explicitly lists session stores as a best-use case, leveraging Redis's fast key-value access patterns for session management.
Redis Streams and Lists enable message queue functionality, explicitly mentioned as a best-use case in the description.
Sorted sets are explicitly mentioned as an advanced data structure that Redis Cloud provides, useful for leaderboards, rankings, and time-series data.
RedisSearch module provides full-text search capabilities, making this a valid provided capability for Redis Cloud deployments with this module enabled.
RedisJSON module enables native JSON document storage and manipulation, making Redis Cloud suitable for document-like workloads.

Redis Cloud is a primary mechanism for distributed state management — session state, feature flags, distributed locks, counters, and leaderboards — making state-management a core output of this pattern.

requires.reasoning: Redis Cloud is a fully-managed service that runs on cloud infrastructure and requires underlying cloud platform resources to operate.
As a cloud-based service, Redis Cloud requires network connectivity for clients to connect and interact with the data store.

eviction_policy.description: Memory eviction strategy when max memory is reached. volatile-* only evicts keys with TTL set (safe for mixed cache/persistent data), allkeys-* evicts any key (pure cache), noeviction returns errors when full

persistence.description: Enable RDB snapshots and AOF logging for data durability. Disable for pure cache use cases where data loss is acceptable for better performance

max_memory_gb.description: Maximum memory allocation in GB. Determines dataset size capacity and cost

replication_enabled.description: Enable primary-replica replication for high availability and automatic failover. Adds cost but provides 99.99% uptime SLA

clustering_enabled.description: Enable Redis Cluster mode for horizontal scaling across multiple nodes. Required for datasets larger than single-node capacity or high throughput needs

tls_enabled.description: Enable TLS encryption for Redis connections. Redis Cloud supports TLS; should be true in all non-local environments.

service_auth_method.description: Redis Cloud connection authentication. 'password' uses a single connection password; 'acl' uses Redis ACL rules with per-user credentials.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Redis Cloud offers a free tier (30MB) suitable for development/testing (min=$0). Small production workloads on 250MB-1GB instances range $5-$50/month. Medium production deployments (5-10GB with high availability) cost $100-$300/month. Large-scale production environments with 50GB+, clustering, multiple availability zones, and Redis modules (RedisJSON, RedisSearch) can reach $500-$800/month. Max set at $800 for typical production scenarios; enterprise multi-region deployments can exceed this.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-low complexity. Redis Cloud is managed, eliminating infrastructure setup overhead. Key adoption costs: (1) Learning Redis data structures and best practices: 8-16 hours ($400-$800 developer time). (2) Migration from existing cache/database if applicable: 4-8 hours ($200-$400). (3) Integration with application code (connection pooling, error handling, serialization): 8-12 hours ($400-$600). (4) Testing and performance tuning: 4-8 hours ($200-$400). Total: 24-44 hours. With AI coding assistants reducing implementation time by 30-40%, estimated at $800 (16-26 effective hours at $50/hour blended rate).
provenance.licenseCost: Redis Cloud operates on a consumption-based SaaS pricing model with no upfront license fees. Free tier available for development. All costs are usage-based (included in monthly operational costs). Redis modules (RedisJSON, RedisSearch, RedisBloom) are included in Redis Cloud pricing without additional licensing. Open-source Redis is Apache/BSD licensed. No separate license purchase required.

supports_nfr.reason: Redis Cloud maximum availability is 99.99% with premium tiers offering active-active replication.
Redis Cloud in-memory operations achieve sub-millisecond latency with network overhead adding 1-2ms floor.
Redis Cloud p99 latency is at least 5ms accounting for network variability and occasional cluster coordination.
Redis Cloud automatic failover typically completes in 1-3 minutes with health monitoring and DNS propagation.
Redis Cloud asynchronous replication maintains RPO of 1-2 minutes under normal conditions due to replication lag.
Redis Cloud can handle up to 100K ops/sec (360M per hour) with clustered deployments and optimized configurations.
Redis Cloud supports extremely high read throughput with clustered sharding and read replicas for key-value operations.
Redis Cloud supports extremely high write throughput with clustered sharding and pipelined write operations.
Redis Cloud supports optional audit logging for compliance scenarios but does not require it for all deployments.
Redis Cloud supports per-database isolation with separate Redis databases or dedicated clusters for multi-tenant architectures.
Redis Cloud can store any data type including PII with appropriate encryption and access controls configured.
Redis Cloud supports GDPR compliance with data residency, encryption, and data deletion capabilities when properly configured.
Redis Cloud offers HIPAA-compliant configurations with BAA, encryption at rest, audit logging, and access controls.
Redis Cloud supports CCPA requirements through data access controls and deletion capabilities with proper configuration.
Redis Cloud can meet SOX requirements with audit logging, access controls, and change tracking when configured appropriately.
Redis Cloud supports read-your-writes consistency when reading from primary node but also allows eventual consistency with replicas.
Redis Cloud is optimized for performance over strict durability with asynchronous persistence and replication by default.

supports_constraints.reason: Redis Cloud is a managed service accessible from any major cloud provider or on-premises environments via network connectivity.
Redis Cloud is accessible via Redis protocol with client libraries available for virtually all programming languages.
Redis Cloud is a backend data store that can be used by any platform type for caching, session management, and data storage.
Pattern provides key-value store functionality as the core Redis data model with string, hash, list, set, and sorted set types.
Pattern specifically requires redis-cloud as the database service
Redis Cloud is commonly used as a caching layer for high-performance data access but also supports primary data storage use cases.
Redis Cloud supports pub/sub and streams for asynchronous messaging patterns with message queuing and event streaming capabilities.
Redis Cloud can be used for AI inference caching and vector storage with RedisJSON and RedisSearch modules for embeddings.
Redis Cloud supports multi-tenancy through per-database isolation or key-based partitioning strategies for tenant separation.
Redis Cloud can support batch processing workflows for caching, temporary storage, and batch job coordination with fast access.
Redis Cloud supports distributed transactions through Redis transactions and Lua scripting for atomic multi-key operations.
Redis Cloud supports real-time streaming with Redis Streams for event processing and time-series data ingestion patterns.
Redis Cloud supports vector search capabilities through RedisSearch module for similarity search and embedding-based retrieval.
Redis Cloud supports document storage through RedisJSON module for JSON document storage and querying with path expressions.
Redis Cloud can support graph operations through RedisGraph module for relationship queries and graph traversal algorithms.
Redis Cloud supports time-series data through RedisTimeSeries module for metric storage and time-based aggregations.
Redis Cloud supports OLTP workloads with fast transactional operations for session stores, caching, and real-time data access.
Redis Cloud can support lightweight OLAP workloads for real-time analytics with aggregations, sorted sets, and RedisSearch.

warn_nfr.reason: Redis Cloud is a managed service with overhead costs. At <10 req/s, simpler storage solutions may be more cost-effective.
warn_nfr.message: db-key-value--redis-cloud: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (<10). Managed Redis Cloud overhead may not be justified at this scale; consider simpler storage options.
